session.user:
Implement the following plan:
# Fix: erkbot `plan list` → `pr list` CLI migration
## Context
`erk plan list` was moved to `erk pr list` in a prior commit (1d0691256), but the erkbot package still invokes the old `["plan", "list", "--all-users"]` CLI args. This causes the bot to fail with "No such command 'list'" when a user types `@erk plan list` in Slack.
The Slack-facing command name (`@erk plan list`) stays the same — users still type `plan list`. Only the internal CLI invocation needs to change to `["pr", "list", ...]`.
## TDD Approach
### Step 1: Update test to assert correct CLI args (RED)
**File:** `packages/erkbot/tests/test_runner.py`
- In `test_run_erk_plan_list_constructs_cli_runner` (line 44): this test patches `click.testing.CliRunner.invoke` directly — when the runner calls `["pr", "list", ...]` instead of `["plan", "list", ...]`, it will still pass (it doesn't assert on args). No change needed here. - In `test_passes_all_users_flag` (line 30): add assertion that the CLI args contain `"pr"` and `"list"` (not `"plan"` and `"list"`). This will fail against the current code.
### Step 2: Fix the runner (GREEN)
**File:** `packages/erkbot/src/erkbot/runner.py` (line 13)
Change: ```python result = await asyncio.to_thread(CliRunner().invoke, cli, ["plan", "list", "--all-users"]) ``` To: ```python result = await asyncio.to_thread(CliRunner().invoke, cli, ["pr", "list", "--all-users"]) ```
### Step 3: Fix the system prompt
**File:** `packages/erkbot/src/erkbot/resources/erk_system_prompt.md`
- Line 9: `uv run erk plan list` → `uv run erk pr list` - Line 17: `uv run erk plan list` → `uv run erk pr list`
### Step 4: Fix system prompt test assertion
**File:** `packages/erkbot/tests/test_prompts.py` (line 16)
Change: ```python self.assertIn("plan list", ERK_SYSTEM_PROMPT) ``` To: ```python self.assertIn("pr list", ERK_SYSTEM_PROMPT) ```
### Step 5: Update Slack handler display text (cosmetic)
**File:** `packages/erkbot/src/erkbot/slack_handlers.py`
Update the user-facing messages on lines 219, 222-224 from `erk plan list` to `erk pr list` so they match the actual command being run. Also update `SUPPORTED_COMMANDS_TEXT` (line 37).
### Step 6: Update handler tests
**File:** `packages/erkbot/tests/test_slack_handlers.py` (line 51-52)
Update the assertion strings from `"erk plan list"` to `"erk pr list"`.
**File:** `packages/erkbot/tests/integration/test_app_mention_integration.py` (lines 44-45)
Update assertion strings from `"plan list"` to `"pr list"`.
## Verification
Run erkbot tests: ``` cd packages/erkbot && uv run pytest tests/ -x ```
If you need specific details from before exiting plan mode (like exact code snippets, error messages, or content you generated), read the full transcript at: /Users/schrockn/.claude/projects/-Users-schrockn--erk-repos-erk-worktrees-add-erkbot-system-prompt/a161c71b-ec1d-4d03-8e78-0c1959f3a295.jsonl
session.thinking: Let me implement this plan step by step. First, let me read all the files I need to modify to understand the current state.
session.assistant: I'll start by reading all the files that need to be modified in parallel.
session.tool_result:
1→import asyncio 2→import unittest 3→from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, MagicMock, patch 4→ 5→from erkbot.runner import run_erk_one_shot, run_erk_plan_list, stream_erk_one_shot 6→ 7→ 8→class TestRunErkPlanList(unittest.IsolatedAsyncioTestCase): 9→ @patch("erkbot.runner.CliRunner") 10→ async def test_strips_ansi_codes_from_output(self, mock_runner_cls: MagicMock) -> None: 11→ """plan list output with ANSI codes (from Rich Console) is stripped.""" 12→ ansi_output = ( 13→ "\x1b[1mpr \x1b[0m\x1b[1m \x1b[0m\x1b[1mstage \x1b[0m\n" 14→ "\x1b]8;;https://github.com/test/repo/issues/1\x1b\\#1\x1b]8;;\x1b\\" 15→ " \x1b[36mimpl\x1b[0m" 16→ ) 17→ invoke_result = MagicMock() 18→ invoke_result.output = ansi_output 19→ invoke_result.exit_code = 0 20→ mock_runner_cls.return_value.invoke.return_value = invoke_result 21→ 22→ result = await run_erk_plan_list() 23→ 24→ self.assertEqual(result.exit_code, 0) 25→ self.assertNotIn("\x1b", result.output) 26→ self.assertIn("pr", result.output) 27→ self.assertIn("#1", result.output) 28→ 29→ @patch("erkbot.runner.CliRunner") 30→ async def test_passes_all_users_flag(self, mock_runner_cls: MagicMock) -> None:
... [105 lines omitted] ...

1→import asyncio 2→from collections.abc import Awaitable, Callable 3→from shutil import which 4→ 5→from click.testing import CliRunner 6→ 7→from erk import cli 8→from erkbot.models import RunResult 9→from erkbot.utils import strip_ansi 10→ 11→ 12→async def run_erk_plan_list() -> RunResult: 13→ result = await asyncio.to_thread(CliRunner().invoke, cli, ["plan", "list", "--all-users"]) 14→ raw_output = result.output or "" 15→ output = strip_ansi(raw_output).strip() or "(no output)" 16→ return RunResult(exit_code=result.exit_code, output=output) 17→ 18→ 19→async def run_erk_one_shot(message: str, *, timeout_seconds: float) -> RunResult: 20→ if which("uv") is None: 21→ return RunResult(exit_code=127, output="Failed to start process: uv was not found in PATH") 22→ 23→ try: 24→ process = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec( 25→ "uv", 26→ "run", 27→ "erk", 28→ "one-shot", 29→ message, 30→ stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
... [70 lines omitted] ...
36→ except TimeoutError: 40→ except TimeoutError: 59→ return RunResult(exit_code=127, output="Failed to start process: uv was not found in PATH") 87→ except TimeoutError: 91→ except TimeoutError:

1→You are erk-bot, an AI assistant for the erk project running in Slack. 2→ 3→You help users check project status, understand plans and objectives, and submit tasks for implementation. You run inside the erk repository with full read access and can execute erk CLI commands. 4→ 5→## Available Commands 6→ 7→Run these via the Bash tool in your working directory: 8→ 9→- `uv run erk plan list` — list open plans with their status 10→- `uv run erk one-shot "<description>"` — submit a task for remote autonomous implementation 11→- `uv run erk dash` — show the objectives dashboard with progress 12→- `uv run erk objective view <number>` — view details for a specific objective 13→ 14→## When to Use Each Command 15→ 16→- **Informational question** (what does X do, explain Y): Answer directly from your knowledge and the codebase. Read files as needed. 17→- **Status check** (what's in progress, show objectives): Run `uv run erk dash` or `uv run erk plan list`. 18→- **Objective details** (tell me about objective 1234): Run `uv run erk objective view <number>`. 19→- **Task requiring code changes** (fix bug X, add feature Y): Run `uv run erk one-shot "<description>"` to submit for remote implementation. Do not make code changes directly. 20→ 21→## Output Formatting 22→ 23→Keep responses concise for Slack: 24→ 25→- No markdown headings — Slack does not render them 26→- Use plain text, `code blocks`, and bullet lists 27→- Keep responses short; prefer a few clear sentences over walls of text 28→- When showing command output, use code blocks 29→ 30→## Limitations
... [5 lines omitted] ...
34→- If a command fails or returns unexpected output, report the error clearly rather than retrying silently.

1→# NOTE: @patch usage is deliberate here. erkbot is a standalone package that 2→# tests third-party Slack SDK wiring and does not use erk's gateway layer. 3→import tempfile 4→import unittest 5→from pathlib import Path 6→ 7→from erkbot.prompts import ERK_SYSTEM_PROMPT, get_erk_system_prompt 8→ 9→ 10→class TestErkSystemPrompt(unittest.TestCase): 11→ def test_erk_system_prompt_loads_from_resources(self) -> None: 12→ self.assertIsInstance(ERK_SYSTEM_PROMPT, str) 13→ self.assertGreater(len(ERK_SYSTEM_PROMPT), 100) 14→ 15→ def test_erk_system_prompt_mentions_key_commands(self) -> None: 16→ self.assertIn("plan list", ERK_SYSTEM_PROMPT) 17→ self.assertIn("one-shot", ERK_SYSTEM_PROMPT) 18→ self.assertIn("dash", ERK_SYSTEM_PROMPT) 19→ self.assertIn("objective view", ERK_SYSTEM_PROMPT) 20→ 21→ def test_get_erk_system_prompt_returns_default(self) -> None: 22→ with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp_dir: 23→ result = get_erk_system_prompt(repo_root=Path(tmp_dir)) 24→ self.assertEqual(result, ERK_SYSTEM_PROMPT) 25→ 26→ def test_get_erk_system_prompt_uses_custom_when_present(self) -> None: 27→ with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp_dir: 28→ tmp_path = Path(tmp_dir) 29→ custom_dir = tmp_path / ".erk" / "prompt-hooks" 30→ custom_dir.mkdir(parents=True)
... [6 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant:
Now the integration test — lines 44-45 use `"plan list"` in `any()` checks which will still match since users type `plan list` and the test text contains `"plan list"`. Let me re-read the integration test assertions more carefully.
The integration test at lines 44-45: ```python assert any("Running" in t and "plan list" in t for t in texts) assert any("Result from" in t and "plan list" in t for t in texts) ```
These check the Slack messages posted by the handler, which now say `"pr list"` instead of `"plan list"`. So these need updating:

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param: assert any("Running" in t and "plan list" in t for t in texts) assert any("Result from" in t and "plan list" in t for t in texts)
assert any("Running" in t and "pr list" in t for t in texts) assert any("Result from" in t and "pr list" in t for t in texts)
